Course Title: AI in Quality Control: When Defects Slip Through
Category: Manufacturing: Chemicals, Food Manufacturing, Industrial Manufacturing, Textiles, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Electronics
Who is this for: Quality Control Managers, Quality Assurance Technicians, Production Supervisors, Manufacturing Engineers, Plant Managers, Quality Inspectors, Operations Managers, Production Coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Quality Assurance, Manufacturing Operations, Production, Quality Control, Engineering, Operations
Description: How vision AI and inspection models can miss critical flaws.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with quality control processes in a manufacturing environment, along with a general curiosity about how artificial intelligence is used in industrial inspection, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the common ways vision AI and automated inspection systems can miss critical defects across manufacturing sectors such as chemicals, food production, pharmaceuticals, and electronics. You will also be able to explain the key factors that contribute to AI inspection failures and apply practical strategies to strengthen quality control processes and reduce the risk of flawed products reaching the market.
